summary: Russian forces reported the liberation of the village Roskoshnoe in the DPR and intensified tactical aviation activity on the north‑east front. Ukraine claimed the destruction of a Russian Geran drone workshop in the Chuguev area and struck the Mariupol port infrastructure. Multiple Shahed attacks were observed over Poltava, while drone strikes hit Crimea, Sevastopol and the Kharkiv region.
key_points: Russian forces liberated the village Roskoshnoe in the DPR, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone‑production workshop in the Chuguev area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukrainian forces destroyed infrastructure at the Mariupol port, reported by the 1st corps of the NUG "Azov" (Source: @UkraineNow/81834).
Shahed (reactive) missiles were repeatedly detected over Poltava from the north, with several alerts posted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67563, @mon1tor_ua/67553).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity on the north‑east direction, warning of possible strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64840).
Ukrainian drones attacked Crimea and Sevastopol overnight, causing fuel supply issues in Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian BBR "Iron Falcons" conducted operations near Temirivka, reported as ongoing (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces liberated Roskoshnoe in the DPR and increased tactical aviation on the north‑east front. Ukraine reported a Geran drone destroying a workshop in Chuguev and struck Mariupol port facilities. Shahed missiles repeatedly targeted Poltava from the north, while Ukrainian drones hit Crimea and Sevastopol.

points: The 1st corps of the NUG "Azov" announced the destruction of Mariupol port infrastructure, indicating a successful strike on a key logistics hub (Source: @UkraineNow/81834).
Ukrainian forces reported a Geran drone destroying a drone‑production workshop in the Chuguev area, reducing Russian UAV capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukrainian BBR "Iron Falcons" conducted operations near Temirivka, with the unit actively engaging enemy forces (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).
Multiple Shahed missiles were detected over Poltava, prompting air defence alerts and indicating continued Russian missile pressure on central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67563, @mon1tor_ua/67553).
Ukrainian drones attacked Crimea and Sevastopol, disrupting fuel deliveries and striking residential and industrial targets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of the village Roskoshnoe in the DPR, expanding control in the Donetsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity on the north‑east direction, warning of possible strikes on frontline oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/64840).
Russian forces reported strikes on Ilyichevsk (Chornomorsk), Zaporizhzhia and Sumy, indicating ongoing offensive operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
A Russian Geran drone was observed over Poltava, suggesting continued use of cruise‑missile UAVs against Ukrainian targets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67559).
Russian forces claimed the use of ballistic "Iskander‑M" missiles from occupied Crimea, highlighting escalation of long‑range strike capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67559).
Ukrainian drones struck targets near Melitopol in the Zaporozhye region, part of a broader campaign against Russian logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian forces reported small‑arms battles in villages such as Bachevsk, Ivolzhanske and Novaya Sich in the Sumy direction, indicating contested ground (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian attacks on the settlement of Klimovo in Bryansk region wounded two civilians, showing cross‑border UAV activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian offensive actions were noted towards Kazachya Lopan in the Kharkiv direction, with assaults in villages Ohrimovka and Losevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a passenger bus on the Melitopol‑Velyka Belozerka route, causing injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east direction, with warnings of possible strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64840).
Ukrainian reconnaissance BpLAs were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, indicating surveillance of the southern coast (Source: @kpszsu/64821).
Ukrainian BpLAs were reported in the Chernihiv region (Rypky) and Sumy region (Konoтop and Stepanivka), showing expanded UAV operations in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/96193).
A Russian drone strike wounded a civilian in the village of Tolpino, Kursk region, demonstrating reach of Ukrainian UAVs into Russian territory (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed missile intercepted over Sumy railway station, with debris falling on a passenger train (Source: @UkraineNow/81832).
Ukrainian drones attacked the Chonhar Bridge on the Kherson‑Crimea border, disrupting traffic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian BpLAs were reported over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, indicating pressure on southern coastal cities (Source: @kpszsu/64821).
Russian strikes hit Ilyichevsk (Chornomorsk) in the Odessa region, showing continued Russian pressure on the southern port (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian forces reported destruction of Russian infrastructure at the Mariupol port, a key southern logistics node (Source: @UkraineNow/81834).
A Russian drone strike wounded civilians in Belgorod region, near Proletarsky, highlighting cross‑border attacks affecting southern Russian oblasts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian drones attacked Crimea and Sevastopol overnight, causing fuel shortages in Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity on the north‑east front, warning of possible strikes on frontline oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/64840).
Several drones were shot down approaching Moscow, indicating heightened air defence activity near the Russian capital (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian FPV drones struck a gas station and shopping centre in Klimovo, Bryansk region, wounding two civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed missile intercepted over Sumy railway station, with debris causing a train fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81832).

key_developments: Russian forces liberated the village Roskoshnoe in the DPR, expanding their control in the Donetsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone‑production workshop in the Chuguev area, reducing Ukraine's UAV manufacturing capacity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukrainian forces destroyed infrastructure at the Mariupol port, striking a key logistical hub (Source: @UkraineNow/81834).
Multiple Shahed missiles were detected over Poltava from the north, prompting air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67563, @mon1tor_ua/67553).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity on the north‑east front, warning of possible strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64840).
Ukrainian drones attacked Crimea and Sevastopol, disrupting fuel supplies and hitting residential targets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian BBR "Iron Falcons" conducted operations near Temirivka, indicating ongoing ground engagements (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are consolidating gains in the Donetsk region while expanding long‑range strike capabilities from occupied Crimea, as indicated by Iskander‑M missile warnings. Ukrainian UAV activity remains high, targeting Russian logistics, port facilities and southern bridges, but Russian tactical aviation pressure on the north‑east front threatens to disrupt Ukrainian rear areas. Both sides are employing a mix of cruise‑missile drones and conventional air strikes, sustaining a high‑intensity, multi‑domain conflict.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to discuss additional air‑defence and missile systems for Ukraine, while internal EU funding disputes persist, reflecting political friction over aid. Russian statements about liberating villages aim to bolster domestic morale and justify continued offensives. Local authorities in affected Ukrainian oblasts report infrastructure damage and civilian casualties, prompting emergency responses and displacement.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Geran and Shahed missile trajectories is critical for early warning of strikes on central Ukraine. The reported liberation of Roskoshnoe suggests possible further advances in the DPR, requiring updated ground‑force assessments. Ukrainian drone production capabilities may be degraded after the Chuguev workshop loss, affecting future UAV sortie rates. Increased Russian tactical aviation activity on the north‑east front warrants heightened air‑defence readiness in Kharkiv and Sumy oblasts.
